description_long: A cluster of three stylized mushrooms is depicted, each with a thick, cylindrical stem and a large, rounded cap. The caps are a vibrant red-orange color, adorned with numerous irregular white spots, reminiscent of the classic Amanita muscaria. The mushrooms are rooted in a circular patch of textured brown earth, suggesting a naturalistic setting. The largest mushroom stands centrally, flanked by two smaller mushrooms, one of which leans outward. The surface of the caps shows a subtle gradient from orange to yellow near the apex, with some speckled dark spots near the base. The stems are smooth and light brown, with no visible gills beneath the caps.
description: A group of three red-orange mushrooms with white spots, growing from a textured brown patch of earth. The largest mushroom is central, with two smaller ones nearby, all featuring smooth stems and rounded caps.
description_view_0: The main view shows the largest mushroom in the center, with a smaller mushroom to the left and another partially visible behind.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the two smaller mushrooms more clearly, with the largest mushroom slightly off-center and the foreground mushroom prominent.
description_view_2: The view focuses on the front of the largest mushroom, with both smaller mushrooms visible and the cap patterns clearly seen.
description_view_3: The largest mushroom is to the right, with the two smaller mushrooms more separated and the earth patch fully visible.
